title: Immediate Advisory: outbound peering disruption — recommended routing & audit steps
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Javier Lugo <ops@acme.ai>, CloudPartner Integrations <partners@cloudpartner.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-21T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Immediate Advisory: outbound peering disruption — recommended routing & audit steps

Hi Javier / team,

TL;DR: We are observing a targeted egress disruption affecting specific peering paths from our us-east and eu-west routing fabric to a small set of third-party cloud peers. Impact is visible as increased error rates and timeouts for requests that traverse those peers — primarily metadata and webhook egress used by some customers.

What we know so far:
- Start time: ~2028-07-21T08:40:00Z.
- Affected regions: us-east (primary) and eu-west (secondary).
- Symptoms: intermittent connection resets, 502/504 spikes for outbound calls to specific cloud peers; token generation and embeddings remain functional except for calls that rely on external webhook callbacks.
- Root cause (investigating): degraded egress peering on a small set of upstream providers. We have traffic telemetry and BGP tracebacks in progress with partners.

Immediate recommendations (what customers should do now):
1) If you control egress routing: apply the attached recommended-reroute-commands.txt to force outbound traffic over alternate peering (fallback-route-1) for the next 2–4 hours.
2) If you rely on webhooks: switch webhook endpoints to the provided relay endpoint (relay-acme.redwood.com) which tunnels outbound calls via an unaffected path.
3) For tenants on dedicated routing policies: opt into our temporary sidecar failover by replying with ACCEPT to this message — we will flip traffic and provide an audit log.

Operational notes and evidence we will provide:
- We will share an audit bundle with per-request traces and BGP tracebacks (attachment: audit-scope-ids.csv).
- A short connectivity diagram is attached showing the fallback topology.
- We will run a post-incident verification sweep and share a remediation schedule within 24 hours.

If you want Redwood to make the changes on your behalf (we can update routing for supported accounts), reply with the preferred contacts and a point of contact for validation.

Apologies for the disruption — we know even short egress interruptions are disruptive. I will keep you updated as we confirm the root cause with the upstream peers.

Attachments: connectivity-diagram.pdf, recommended-reroute-commands.txt, audit-scope-ids.csv

--
Alex Martinez
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1-415-555-0192
https://redwood.com


From: Javier Lugo <ops@acme.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudPartner Integrations <partners@cloudpartner.com>
Date: 2028-07-21T09:37:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Immediate Advisory: outbound peering disruption — recommended routing & audit steps

Thanks Alex — quick questions:
1) Can you paste the exact iptables/nexthop commands we should run? Our infra team is on call and prefers copy/paste.
2) Does this affect model inference traffic (generation/embeddings) or just outbound webhooks/third-party integrations?
3) ETA for logs? We need them for our incident report.

We can give you ACCEPT to apply changes if you can include a short blast radius window and rollback steps.

> TL;DR: We are observing a targeted egress disruption affecting specific peering paths from our us-east and eu-west routing fabric to a small set of third-party cloud peers.
>
> [attachment: recommended-reroute-commands.txt]

--
Javier Lugo
Head of Infrastructure, Acme AI
ops@acme.ai

To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-21T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Immediate Advisory: outbound peering disruption — internal actions

Forwarding external thread — immediate internal action items below.

=== Forwarded message ===
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Javier Lugo <ops@acme.ai>
Date: 2028-07-21T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Immediate Advisory: outbound peering disruption — recommended routing & audit steps

(see original advisory)

Internal action list (owners):
- Markus: validate the recommended-reroute-commands.txt on staging and produce a one-line rollback command. ETA 30m.
- Monica: prepare the audit bundle with per-request traces for all affected accounts (start by Acme AI). Put logs onto drive link and include HBSP-48392 in the bundle. ETA 90m.
- Ben: coordinate account approvals and confirm which accounts want Redwood to enact the change. Draft a short opt-in confirmation message we can send to customers. ETA 45m.
- Alex: keep customer comms and run the live validation call for Acme if they accept.

Notes: prioritize accounts with active webhooks first. We should also prepare a limited credit offer for impacted customers — Ben please align with finance if needed.

Attachments referenced: connectivity-diagram.pdf, recommended-reroute-commands.txt

--
Alex Martinez


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Javier Lugo <ops@acme.ai>
Cc: CloudPartner Integrations <partners@cloudpartner.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-22T14:10:00-07:00
Subject: Resolved (partial): reroute confirmed; next steps for post-incident review

Hi Javier — quick update and next steps following your ACCEPT earlier today.

What we did:
- At 2028-07-21T10:20:00Z we flipped routes for Acme AI to fallback-route-1 (confirmed by traceroutes attached).
- 10:30–11:15Z: Monitoring showed request success rates returning to baseline for calls that previously failed over the affected peers.

Current status:
- Service: primary service and model inference are healthy; the impact was limited to outbound webhook/egress calls routed via the affected peers.
- Status: mitigated (routing fallback in place). Upstream provider is being engaged for formal RCA.

Deliverables we will provide within 24 hours:
1) Audit bundle (drive link above) with per-request traces and BGP tracebacks.
2) Postmortem draft including timeline, root cause (when confirmed), and long-term mitigations. Proposed review window: 2028-07-23T16:00:00-07:00 — would you like to join?
3) Rollback recipe and verification checklist (attached).

If you still see anomalies please reply with the exact request ids (we included a short snippet of request ids in audit-scope-ids.csv).

Thanks again for the quick turnaround and for letting us enable the fallback. We will continue to press the upstream provider for a timeline and will share an RCA as soon as available.

Attachments: traceroute-results.txt, audit-scope-ids.csv

--
Alex Martinez
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1-415-555-0192

title: Blue Harbor: sudden P95/P99 spike — suspected inter-region egress mixup
preview:
From: Alex Jenkins <alex.jenkins@blueharbor.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: support@blueharbor.ai
Date: 2028-07-22T09:11:00-04:00
Subject: Sudden P95/P99 spike for our EU traffic — started 07/21 03:00 UTC

Hi Ben / Laura,

We're seeing a large jump in tail latency on our calls to the hosted API for EU-region keys. P95 has moved from ~120ms to ~1.1s and P99 is spiking above 2.5s. Error rate (502/504) is also up — around 1.8% over the last 6 hours. This started roughly after 2028-07-21 03:00 UTC (we correlated with our request timestamps).

Subset of example request IDs: BH-REQ-2412, BH-REQ-2430, BH-REQ-2478 (timestamps attached in the log snippet). Our SLAs are sensitive to p95 and a few production paths are timing out. Can you confirm whether there was any routing/egress change pushed recently? Please escalate if needed — this is affecting two of our customers in EU.

I attached a small CSV with sample request timestamps. Happy to hop on a quick call.

Thanks,
Alex
Senior Platform Engineer
Blue Harbor Analytics
attachments: request-sample-07122.csv


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Jenkins <alex.jenkins@blueharbor.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-22T09:20:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Sudden P95/P99 spike for our EU traffic — started 07/21 03:00 UTC

Alex — thanks for the heads up and the request IDs. I'm escalating this to our SRE and ops team now (Laura), and looping in Marissa for exec visibility.

Immediate ask to you: can you confirm a few more details — mainly whether the traffic is coming from multiple EU locations (we think yes) and whether you can share traceroutes to any slow request IDs (if convenient). We'll triage and try to isolate if this is an internal routing or your peering.

Laura — can you look at egress routing configs for EU tenants around 2028-07-21T03:00Z? If we rolled a policy that caused an EU->US egress path, that explains the RTT jump. If confirmed, we should consider rolling back or manually pinning Blue Harbor to EU egress.

I can join a quick bridge if useful.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Alex Jenkins <alex.jenkins@blueharbor.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-22T09:45:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Sudden P95/P99 spike for our EU traffic — started 07/21 03:00 UTC

Alex / Ben — quick update from SRE triage.

What I checked so far:
- Change log shows a rollout of an egress routing policy (internal ref: rollout-id eg-2028-07-21-03) that adjusted failover precedence between EU edge clusters and pooled US egress for cost-balancing.
- Canary metrics at rollout time didn't show errors, but the policy contained a region-tag mismatch for a subset of accounts — Blue Harbor's tenant tag was evaluated incorrectly and matched the US egress bucket.
- Traceroute samples (attached) show path length EU-client -> EU-edge -> US-egress -> model-host (extra inter-region hop) which explains additional RTT of ~700–900ms on the tail.

Attached: traceroute-sample-0718.txt, p95-trend-2028-07-20-22.png (screenshots of the spike).

Immediate mitigation options:
1) Roll back rollout-id eg-2028-07-21-03 (fast but requires verifying no downstream regressions) — ETA ~25–40 minutes to fully propagate.
2) Pin Blue Harbor traffic to EU egress manually (config override) — ETA ~15–25 minutes and lower blast radius.
3) Emergency regional fallback: force route for high-latency requests to fallback model variant in EU (adds complexity).

I recommend option 2 first (pin), verify p95, then roll back policy if needed. I'll start the override now pending Ben/Marissa sign-off.

— Laura
Laura Bennett
SRE Manager, Redwood Inference
attachments: traceroute-sample-0718.txt; p95-trend-2028-07-20-22.png


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-22T10:05:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Sudden P95/P99 spike for our EU traffic — suspected inter-region egress mixup

Looping Rishi and Stephanie since this is a customer-impacting outage with possible contractual implications. Thanks Laura for the quick triage. Ben — please confirm Blue Harbor is amenable to the manual egress pin while we validate.

Rishi: if we proceed with pinning and it resolves p95, please prioritize a rollback gate review and timeline for a root-cause postmortem. Stephanie: please prepare customer remediation options (service credit band, ETA communications, internal incident channel).

I want a short status every 30 minutes until we see p95 converge back to baseline. If pin doesn't fix it, escalate to rollback.

— Marissa
Marissa Cole
VP, Customer Reliability


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>

Subject: Sudden P95/P99 spike for our EU traffic — started 07/21 03:00 UTC

Hi Ben / Laura,

We're seeing a large jump in tail latency on our calls to the hosted API for EU-region keys. P95 has moved from ~120ms to ~1.1s and P99 is spiking above 2.5s. Error rate (502/504) is also up — around 1.8% over the last 6 hours. This started roughly after 2028-07-21 03:00 UTC (we correlated with our request timestamps).

Subset of example request IDs: BH-REQ-2412, BH-REQ-2430, BH-REQ-2478 (timestamps attached in the log snippet). Our SLAs are sensitive to p95 and a few production paths are timing out. Can you confirm whether there was any routing/egress change pushed recently? Please escalate if needed — this is affecting two of our customers in EU.

I attached a small CSV with sample request timestamps. Happy to hop on a quick call.

Thanks,
Alex
Senior Platform Engineer
Blue Harbor Analytics

Subject: Re: Sudden P95/P99 spike for our EU traffic — started 07/21 03:00 UTC

Alex — thanks for the heads up and the request IDs. I'm escalating this to our SRE and ops team now (Laura), and looping in Marissa for exec visibility.

Immediate ask to you: can you confirm a few more details — mainly whether the traffic is coming from multiple EU locations (we think yes) and whether you can share traceroutes to any slow request IDs (if convenient). We'll triage and try to isolate if this is an internal routing or your peering.

Laura — can you look at egress routing configs for EU tenants around 2028-07-21T03:00Z? If we rolled a policy that caused an EU->US egress path, that explains the RTT jump. If confirmed, we should consider rolling back or manually pinning Blue Harbor to EU egress.

I can join a quick bridge if useful.

Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Alex Jenkins <alex.jenkins@blueharbor.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-22T09:45:00-04:00
Subject: Re: Sudden P95/P99 spike for our EU traffic — started 07/21 03:00 UTC

Alex / Ben — quick update from SRE triage.

What I checked so far:
- Change log shows a rollout of an egress routing policy (internal ref: rollout-id eg-2028-07-21-03) that adjusted failover precedence between EU edge clusters and pooled US egress for cost-balancing.
- Canary metrics at rollout time didn't show errors, but the policy contained a region-tag mismatch for a subset of accounts — Blue Harbor's tenant tag was evaluated incorrectly and matched the US egress bucket.
- Traceroute samples (attached) show path length EU-client -> EU-edge -> US-egress -> model-host (extra inter-region hop) which explains additional RTT of ~700–900ms on the tail.

Attached: traceroute-sample-0718.txt, p95-trend-2028-07-20-22.png (screenshots of the spike).

Immediate mitigation options:
1) Roll back rollout-id eg-2028-07-21-03 (fast but requires verifying no downstream regressions) — ETA ~25–40 minutes to fully propagate.
2) Pin Blue Harbor traffic to EU egress manually (config override) — ETA ~15–25 minutes and lower blast radius.
3) Emergency regional fallback: force route for high-latency requests to fallback model variant in EU (adds complexity).

I recommend option 2 first (pin), verify p95, then roll back policy if needed. I'll start the override now pending Ben/Marissa sign-off.

— Laura
Laura Bennett
SRE Manager, Redwood Inference
attachments: traceroute-sample-0718.txt; p95-trend-2028-07-20-22.png

title: Re: Acme Health - unwind protection & ramp flexibility request
preview:
From: Megan Alvarez <megan@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@acmehealth.com
Date: 2028-07-19T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Acme - request for ramp flexibility and early termination protections

Ben / Karthik —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our execs want to move forward with a 12-month committed plan, but they asked for a bit more downward flexibility up front given uncertain utilization across a couple of regions. Two specific asks:

- A soft "downsell" option allowing us to reduce committed spend by up to 50% once during the first 90 days with limited penalty.
- An early termination protection (unwind credit) in case we need to step out entirely within the first 6 months.

I attached a short note with Acme's preferred language (Acme_Unwind_Proposal_v1.pdf). Can your team confirm whether Redwood can offer something like this, and what approvals are needed? If there are revenue implications we can take that to procurement/finance — wanted to surface here so we can keep the timeline.

Goal: sign before end of July if we can get terms worked.

Best,
Megan
Megan Alvarez
Director, Strategic Programs
Acme Health

---
Attachment: Acme_Unwind_Proposal_v1.pdf (proposal stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-07-19T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Acme - request for ramp flexibility and early termination protections

Looping you both in — Acme is asking for a one-time downsizing option + an unwind credit. They want to close fast (end of month).

Summary of asks:
- Downsell: reduce committed monthly spend by up to 50% once within first 90 days, no automatic penalty described in their doc.
- Unwind: early termination within first 6 months with a proposed credit equal to 25% of remaining committed value.

Monica: can you run a north-star revenue impact calc? Karthik / Laura — want your take on whether we can greenlight a counterproposal and what approval band we should take to Deal Desk / Revenue Ops.

I suggest we offer a carveout with a capped unwind credit and a 30-day notice requirement to prevent churn stacking. Draft below for your review.

Ben

---
Attachment: redwood_term_sheet.docx (term sheet stub)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-07-20T09:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Acme - request for ramp flexibility and early termination protections

Thanks — ran numbers with Monica. Proposal (revenue owner + legal-friendly language):

Counterproposal (high level):
1) Downsell protection: Customer may elect, once in the first 90 days after contract start, to reduce committed monthly spend by up to 50%. Reduction takes effect 30 days after notice. When a downsell is elected the customer will forfeit any unused prepaid credits for the month of change; ongoing committed rate will be adjusted prospectively.

2) Early termination (unwind) protection: If Customer terminates the Agreement within the first 180 days, Redwood will provide a one-time unwind credit equal to 20% of the remaining committed monthly fees (capped at the equivalent of 3 months' fees). Credit is applied against final invoice. Customer must provide 30 days' written notice.

Rationale: 20% / 3-month cap keeps signal alignment with committed revenue while providing a meaningful concession for their uncertainty. This is revenue-approved at SR level but will need Exec signoff if credit/cap deviates.

Ask to Laura: approve giving sales authority to offer the above as a one-time concession for Acme Health (deal DL-2027-1147). If approved, Legal will formalize language in amendment and Finance needs to confirm accounting treatment.

If Laura approves here, we can present to Megan tomorrow and push for end-of-month signature.

Karthik
Head of Deal Desk

---
Quoted original message below:
> From: Megan Alvarez <megan@acmehealth.com>
> Date: Thu, Jul 19, 2028 at 8:12 AM
> Subject: Acme - request for ramp flexibility and early termination protections
> 
> Ben / Karthik —
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week...

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-07-21T11:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Acme - request for ramp flexibility and early termination protections

Karthik — thanks for the thoughtful counterproposal. From a revenue stewardship perspective I can approve the language with the following edits and conditions:

- Approve the downsell option as written (one-time in first 90 days, 30-day effective notice).
- Reduce unwind credit to 20% capped at 3 months' fees (as you proposed) — this is approved.
- Require a 30-day notice for termination (included).
- Request Finance confirm no unusual ASC treatment; do not sign until Finance confirms.
- Legal should add a clause preventing immediate re-commitment to a lower tier to prevent churn game-playing (one 12-month recommitment window after a downsell).

Monica — please attach the 120/365 revenue impact and confirm the credit cap is OK from ops and accounting. If Finance backs this, consider this Exec-approved for Acme Health only (non-precedential). Ben / Karthik — proceed to put final language in an amendment and send to Megan; flag Legal + Finance review.

Laura Bennett
VP, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Reply inline:
> Karthik: if Finance signs off I approve. Add non-precedential language.

Ben: please coordinate the amendment and keep me cc'd on final redline.

title: Proposal: compressed exec demo slot due to CIO travel — options and standby coverage
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novaworks.com>; Samir Patel <samir.patel@novaworks.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Jul 2028 14:12:00 -0700
Subject: Kickoff demo — exec attendance question and contingency options

Hi Elena / Samir,

Thanks again for booking time next week for the platform demo. Quick note from our side — we want to confirm whether your CIO will be able to join the full 45-minute slot or if travel constraints might require compression. We want to be flexible and have a couple of proposals so we can make the most of the exec(s) time: 

- Option A (preferred): Keep the 45-minute session with CIO + Product VP (full demo + Q&A).
- Option B: Compress to a 25-minute exec highlight (top two ROI slides + 10 min live demo) and follow with a 30-minute technical deep dive for the engineering team later the same day.
- Option C: Maintain original 45-minute calendar but nominate a VP-level delegate for the first 20 minutes, with CIO joining for the final 15 minutes (we’ll hold a hard handoff).

If travel forces a late arrival, we can also provide an unlisted recording (you can opt out if preferred) and a 15-minute executive recap. I’ve attached an updated agenda and a mutual action plan for visibility.

Could you let me know which of the three options would work best by EOD Thursday PST? If none, send two times that work for the CIO and we’ll propose a replacement slot.

Thanks — Ben

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Director | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0192

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Ben — thanks for the clear options. A couple of asks from our side: 
- Can you guarantee the first 10 minutes focus strictly on ROI + deployment risk mitigations? That’s what the CIO cares about.

We prefer no recording of the executive segment — feel free to record the engineering deep dive.

Elena

title: Private deployment connectivity intake & verification
preview:
From: Samuel Park <samuel.park@nimble-rx.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops+infra@nimble-rx.com
Date: Thu, 12 Jul 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Connectivity intake for private deployment

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday. We're ready to start the network & environment intake for the Private deployment (VPC + on-prem edge). To make sure we don't miss anything from a security and audit perspective, can you send the exact prereqs and the evidence artifacts you need for acceptance?

What we can share today:
- nimble_network_diagram_v2.pdf (attached)
- Our SAML metadata and IDP contact (attached sso_metadata.xml)
- A short inventory of public/private subnets and responsible owners (will follow by EOD)

Immediate asks:
1) A checklist of mandatory items (ports, VPN/IPSEC, routes, KMS requirements, cert expectations)
2) Verification steps you run for environment acceptance (smoke tests, sample traffic patterns, telemetry checks)
3) Any timeline constraints to avoid disrupting our change window next week (7/18–7/19)

We want the mutual action plan and evidence log captured so our auditors can sign off quickly. If possible, can we get the template or spreadsheet you prefer we populate?

Appreciate it — happy to hop on a short call to review.

—Samuel
Samuel Park
Head of Platform Ops, NimbleRx
samuel.park@nimble-rx.com
Mobile: +1 415-555-0199

Attachments: nimble_network_diagram_v2.pdf (diagram, 1.8MB), sso_metadata.xml (SAML metadata)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samuel Park <samuel.park@nimble-rx.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 12 Jul 2028 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Connectivity intake for private deployment

Hi Samuel — thanks for the packet and for laying out the asks.

I'll kick this off from our side and attach the intake template (redwood_connectivity_template.xlsx) for your team to fill. Quick summary of what we'll need from you in the template:
- IP ranges (public egress and private VPC ranges)
- NAT/firewall rules and CIDR-to-service mapping
- SAML issuer/ACS endpoints + test account details
- KMS key TTL and whether we should expect CMK rotation windows

Verification items we'll run (high level):
- Connectivity: SYN/ACK smoke between your edge & our staging ingress (TCP ports 443, 10250, 5000 for metrics)
- Routing: BGP/Static route validation, route propagation checks
- Security: SAML auth flow test, signed JWT validation, KMS encrypt/decrypt sample
- Telemetry: sample trace ingestion and 24h retention confirmation

We're available for a 30m intake call tomorrow (7/13) at 10:00 PT if that works. If your change window on 7/18–19 is tight, let's aim to complete verification by 7/16.

I've CC'd Kimberly (security lead) so she can flag any additional evidence needed for compliance.

Regards,
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood_connectivity_template.xlsx (please populate and return)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 13 Jul 2028 08:25:00 -0700
Subject: NRX intake — checklist + verification plan (internal)

Team — forwarding Samuel's intake and the diagram they provided. Summary and proposed gating checklist below; marking items we need to own versus artifacts the customer provides.

Gating checklist (minimum viable acceptance):
- Network connectivity
  - Customer provides: public egress IPs, private VPC CIDRs, edge NAT mapping (artifact)
  - Our task: perform TCP smoke (443), metrics port checks (10250), and ensure route reachability

- VPN / Interconnect
  - Customer provides: VPN config or direct connect details; PSKs for IPSEC test account
  - Our task: establish test tunnel, verify MTU/path MTU, test flow stability for 15 minutes

- Firewall / ACLs
  - Customer provides: outbound allowlist and maintenance windows
  - Our task: test curl requests to health endpoints and confirm egress is not blocked by DPI

- Identity & Keys
  - Customer provides: SAML metadata (done), test user credentials, KMS ARN and policy docs
  - Our task: run SAML sign-on test, verify KMS encrypt/decrypt, check rotation policy compatibility

- Telemetry and Logging
  - Customer provides: where to ship metrics/logs (endpoint and auth key)
  - Our task: ingest sample traces, validate retention and retention proof artifacts (syslog/stackdriver export)

Evidence log (what we capture):
- network_test_results-<date>.csv (tcp/icmp results)
- saml_flow_screenshot.png (login flow capture)
- kms_encrypt_test.txt (base64 cipher & decrypt confirmation)

Operational notes / constraints:
- To meet their 7/18 change window, we need all artifacts back by EOD 7/14 and confirmation of a 7/15 internal smoke slot.
- If KMS requires HSM on their side, escalate to Product for key wrapping support.

Karthik — can you confirm we have a staging ingress in us-west that can accept test traffic for NRX by 7/15? Marissa — can you schedule 7/15 09:00–10:00 PT for smoke validation and invite the customer? Kimberly — any extra evidence you'd require from a compliance standpoint?

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Samuel Park <samuel.park@nimble-rx.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: ops+infra@nimble-rx.com
Date: Tue, 16 Jul 2028 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Connectivity intake for private deployment

Thanks Ben — that template looks perfect. We've filled the spreadsheet and attached the completed version to the HubSpot deal (link below).

A few quick confirmations: 
- We returned the completed redwood_connectivity_template.xlsx and confirmed our change window is flexible on 7/15 if that helps.
- We'd prefer the 09:00 PT smoke slot on 7/15.
- KMS is customer-managed; we can provide a temporary test key for the encrypt/decrypt sample.

I've shared the drive folder with the artifacts and given Kimberly admin access so she can pull logs if needed. Please send the calendar invite for 7/15 09:00 PT and the temporary test account details you need for SAML.

Look forward to the run; we'll keep ops+infra on the thread for any immediate troubleshooting.

—Samuel
Samuel Park
Head of Platform Ops, NimbleRx

Related links: HubSpot deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deal/4812), Shared drive: https://drive.nimble-rx.com/shared/NRX-onboard-2028-07-12

title: Marketplace: data locality & record lifecycle accountability — Cloudfolio onboarding
preview:
From: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, legal@cloudfolio.com
Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the onboarding call yesterday. As Cloudfolio prepares its listing, Legal and Ops need a short Q&A we can attach to the contract. Two quick items we can’t leave ambiguous:

1) Deletion trigger ownership — when a customer terminates or requests data deletion via Cloudfolio, who (Cloudfolio or Redwood) is responsible for executing deletion across persisted storage and any caching layers? We need a step-by-step flow to include in the SLA.

2) Multi-cloud locality — our customers will choose regions across AWS us-west-2, GCP us-central1, and Azure eastus. We need confirmation that deletion will be carried out in the data’s home region(s) and that logs/audit evidence will be provided per-region.

Attaching our initial list of contract requirements for reference. Can you provide 1–2 bullet paragraphs per item we can hand to our legal team? Also let us know if Redwood needs any contract language specific to marketplace listing or if you’ll accept Cloudfolio as the single point of contact for deletion requests.

Appreciate a quick turnaround — our legal lead wants to close the listing by the end of next week.

Thanks,
Priya Singh
Head of Platform Operations
Cloudfolio

Attachments: onboarding_requirements.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Priya —

Thanks — understood. Below are concise answers you can drop into the Q&A for legal. I’ve also attached an updated SLA excerpt (Cloudfolio_Data_SLA_draft_v2.pdf) with suggested language and an architecture sketch (deletion_flow_v1.png).

Short answers you can include now:
- Deletion trigger ownership: Cloudfolio is the primary requester (single point of contact). Redwood executes the deletion across our managed runtime and any persistent storage we control. We provide a deletion confirmation with per-region audit entries to Cloudfolio.
- Multi-cloud locality: For hosted marketplace listings, data persists only in the region selected at customer creation. Redwood will execute deletion within the same region(s). For Dedicated/Private deployments we can tie deletion behavior to the customer’s deployment model — language in the SLA is different there (see attached).

Next steps:
1) Jordan — can your team confirm the list of endpoints (API/webhook) we’ll expose for deletion requests and the expected JSON payload?
2) Marissa — can you add brief technical notes on our retained cache vs persisted storage and the expected completion window per region?

If you want, I can convene a 30-minute sync with Legal + Ops next week to agree on wording.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Partnerships
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Cloudfolio_Data_SLA_draft_v2.pdf

On Sat, Jul 14, 2028 at 09:12 AM Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com> wrote:
> [original message]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Priya Singh <priya.singh@cloudfolio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Jul 2028 12:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding — data lifecycle & deletion responsibilities

Hi Priya — adding technical detail to what Ben outlined so legal has clear, testable commitments.

Operational model (hosted marketplace listing):
- Ingestion and ephemeral model runtime: inference requests may produce transient artifacts (KV cache, model context) that are kept in GPU-local memory or ephemeral stores for the duration of the session and are not persisted beyond configured TTLs (default 24 hours) unless explicitly stored by the customer. Those ephemeral artifacts are deleted by the runtime immediately after TTL expiry or session termination.

- Persistent storage: any customer-uploaded files or logs are written to region-specific object storage (e.g., S3 in us-west-2, GCS in us-central1, Azure Blob in eastus). Deletion request flow for persisted objects:
  1) Confirm request origin (Cloudfolio admin webhook or signed API request).
  2) Mark records for deletion in metadata index (fast), enqueue region-specific deletion job.
  3) Execute object store deletion + tombstone propagation to CDN/edge caches.
  4) Run verification job that checks object absence and record hash mismatch (audit proof).
  5) Emit per-region audit entry (timestamp, object ids, job id, deletion status).

- Cross-region considerations: if a customer has replicas in multiple regions, Redwood will delete replicas in each region where data is stored. Our SLA language will define \"per-region completion\" windows (we propose: 30 days for full verification across all persisted replicas; faster for non-replicated single-region objects).

- Proof of deletion: we can provide a signed audit bundle (JSON lines) containing object ids, deletion job ids, timestamps, and verification hashes. For marketplace listings we propose delivering this via secure S3 link and a signed email to Cloudfolio ops. For Dedicated/Private customers, proof-of-deletion can be delivered to the customer’s configured audit endpoint.

- Exceptions & retention holds: Redwood will honor court orders/retention holds. The SLA needs language on how those holds affect deletion timelines. We’ll also need to capture whether Cloudfolio will act as the custodian of legal holds or if Redwood needs to escalate holds through a legal contact.

Clock/latency expectations:
- Fast-path (single-region, no legal hold): deletion job scheduled within 1 hour, bulk verification complete within 48 hours for small datasets, up to 30 days for large replicated datasets.

I can convert the bullets above into contractual language (two paragraphs + definitions) once we settle the per-region completion window.

-Marissa
Sr. Product Manager, Platform
Redwood Inference

On Sat, Jul 14, 2028 at 10:03 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:

Cloudfolio Ops

title: Urgent: P95/P99 surge and TPS choke - FastApp production
preview:
From: Samir Joshi <samir@fastapp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@fastapp.com
Date: 2028-07-15T08:12:00Z
Subject: Production: sudden p95/p99 latency spike + 40% token/sec drop

Ben —

We started seeing a sudden regression in production starting ~07:55 UTC: p95 for text-generation calls has spiked from ~220ms to 1.2s, p99 to ~2.4s. Token-per-second (throughput) for our main /v1/generate route has dropped ~35-45% depending on sequence length. Users are seeing timeouts and conversion is impacted.

Quick details:
- Region: us-east-1
- Affected model family: redwood-v2-large (hosted)
- Error rate: 0.7% -> 1.9% (mostly upstream timeouts)
- Window: 07:55 - now (continuous)

We are in a live campaign launch and need urgent help. Can you confirm this is on your side and provide ETA for mitigation?

Samir
Samir Joshi
Head of Platform Ops, FastApp

Attachments: perf-snap-0755.png (screenshot)


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samir Joshi <samir@fastapp.com>
Cc: Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-15T08:18:00Z
Subject: Re: Production: sudden p95/p99 latency spike + 40% token/sec drop

Samir — thanks for the alert and the screenshots. We're treating this as high priority. I've looped in infra and product.

Immediate asks:
- Hiro, can you confirm whether there were any runtime/kernel/quantization changes in the past 2 hours for the redwood-v2-large family in us-east-1?
- Monica/Laura, we should prepare an external-facing status update for FastApp and options (rollback/route to dedicated).

Samir, we'll follow up within 30 minutes with a triage summary and next steps.

Ben Carter
Account Exec — Redwood Inference


From: Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-15T08:32:00Z
Subject: Re: Production: sudden p95/p99 latency spike + 40% token/sec drop

Quick triage notes from infra:

What we see:
- Canary rollout of runtime 2.3.1 (kernel selection changes + default int8 quant path) began at 07:40 UTC for a subset of hosted instances in us-east-1.
- Telemetry shows a sharp divergence for redwood-v2-large on quantized kernels: tokens/sec down ~35% during the rollout window, p95/p99 increased coincidentally.
- KV-cache hit patterns look similar; not an obvious cache miss storm.
- Error rate increase is correlated with timeouts on slower quantized paths under tail latency.

Artifacts attached: canary-rollout-log.txt, token_profile_delta.csv (comparison of tokens/sec distributions). Dashboards: https://redwood.internal/dashboards/throughput?run=8321

Immediate options:
1) Pause/rollback the canary to 2.3.0 kernel selection for affected shards (fastest mitigation).
2) Enable fallback to FP16 kernel for redwood-v2-large for customer traffic (requires config flip).
3) Route FastApp traffic to non-canary pool (if capacity available).

I recommend (1) + (3) in parallel to stop user impact quickly while we continue root-cause. If we rollback the canary, we should expect metrics to normalize within 10-20 minutes post-rollback.

Hiro
Senior Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-15T08:45:00Z
Subject: Re: Production: sudden p95/p99 latency spike + 40% token/sec drop

Adding product context — thanks Hiro. A couple things to note for the customer-facing message:
- We should be explicit that this looks linked to a kernel+quant path selection change in the runtime canary (not a model weight change).
- Recommend we offer FastApp a) immediate mitigation (rollback + routing) b) a credit for the impact window c) postmortem + timeline for fix.

I'll draft a short status note for Samir once we decide on mitigation. Ben, please confirm whether you want me to include an ETA figure or keep it "in progress" until the rollback is complete.

Laura Bennett
Product Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samir Joshi <samir@fastapp.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-15T09:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Production: sudden p95/p99 latency spike + 40% token/sec drop

Samir — short update: we've identified a likely root cause in a canaryed kernel/quantization path and are preparing an immediate mitigation. Options we're executing now: pause the canary and route FastApp traffic to stable pools. We expect to start the mitigation within ~10 minutes and to see improvements within 20 minutes after that.

We're also preparing a credit proposal for the impacted window; Monica will follow up with specifics. I'll send another note once metrics start to improve or sooner if the plan changes.

Ben Carter
Account Exec — Redwood Inference


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-15T12:03:00Z
Subject: Mitigation completed — partial rollback + routing applied

Status: mitigation executed at 11:22 UTC. Actions taken:
- Canary paused and affected kernel selection rolled back to 2.3.0 for redwood-v2-large in us-east-1.
- FastApp traffic routed to stable non-canary pools for the affected endpoints.
- Temporary alert thresholds lowered to detect any residual tail regressions.

Effect: p95 reduced from 1.2s -> ~260ms, p99 reduced from 2.4s -> ~420ms, tokens/sec returned to within ~5% of baseline for the majority of observed requests. Error rate returned to ~0.8%.

Next steps:
- Conduct a controlled A/B perf test across sequence lengths and KV-cache scenarios to reproduce the tokens/sec delta on quant kernels (assigned to Hiro).
- Prepare an internal postmortem with timeline and decision log; intended owner: Laura.
- Customer cadence: Ben/Monica to send formal impact credit proposal and schedule a post-incident review with FastApp.

Rishi Malhotra
Engineering Lead, Serving Runtime — Redwood Inference

title: Residency & retention carveouts — proposed DPA language and Q&A
preview:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acmeinc.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Jul 2028 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Questions on backup/archive carveouts in DPA (retention/deletion)

Hi Ava / Karthik,

Thanks for sending the draft DPA excerpt earlier. Our security and legal teams reviewed the retention & deletion section and surfaced a couple of carveout questions we need clarified before we can sign:

1) Backups / snapshots: We need explicit language around certified erasure for backups that live outside the primary region (onshore exports, disaster recovery). Can Redwood provide an operational SLA or evidence types (forensics log, wipe certificate) after an erasure request?

2) Telemetry/ephemeral cache: The draft says telemetry is retained for X days. We need a tighter window for any logs that may contain customer identifiers (30 days max) and confirmation those logs are truncated/anonymized before cross-border transfer.

3) BYOK and KMS: If we use BYOK in our EU tenant but have optional US failover, will Redwood still be able to perform onshore forensic access if requested by law enforcement? We need the residency language to reflect controlled onshore access only with customer approval (and an audit trail).

We attached our annotated comments as a PDF (acme_dpa_comments.pdf). Can you propose redline language for the backup and telemetry carveouts and an evidence matrix for erasure?

Appreciate a short call if needed.

- Lena
Senior InfoSec Manager
Acme Inc.
attachments: acme_dpa_comments.pdf


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acmeinc.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Acme Legal <contracts@acmeinc.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Jul 2028 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on backup/archive carveouts in DPA (retention/deletion)

Lena — thanks for the clear notes and the attachment. Quick summary and what well propose: 

- Certified erasure for backups: Redwood supports a certified erasure flow for backups and snapshots in scope of the DPA. Operationally we can provide a "Erase Confirmation" (signed PDF) and a log bundle showing the purge job id, snapshot ids, and storage tombstone entries. SLA: target 30 business days for proofs for on-demand erasure requests for backups, 60 business days if cross-region coordination is required.

- Telemetry & ephemeral caches: We can commit to a 30-day retention for telemetry containing customer identifiers by default and provide a redaction/anonymization note before cross-border moves. For telemetry that is required for product ops wed propose an explicit exception list.

- BYOK / onshore forensic access: By default, BYOK keys remain under customer control. Well add language that any onshore access to decrypted customer data for law enforcement requires: (a) customer approval OR (b) a lawful access escalation with documented notice and an audit trail to the customer within 72 hours. We cannot unilaterally access BYOK-managed keys without the customer's private key.

Im attaching a proposed redline (DPA_proposed_redlines_20280712.docx) and a short retention/evidence matrix (retention_evidence_matrix.xlsx) that maps the categories (backups, MR snapshot, ephemeral telemetry, logs) to retention windows and expected evidence types. Please see the "Backup & Snapshot" section (p.3) for a sample clause that Karthik suggested.

If that looks broadly acceptable, we can update the DPA and circulate the DocuSign link. Happy to hop on a 30-minute call — suggest tomorrow 09:00 PT if that works for you.

Thanks,

Ava Chen
Product Counsel, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123
Redwood Inference
attachments: DPA_proposed_redlines_20280712.docx; retention_evidence_matrix.xlsx


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acmeinc.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Jul 2028 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on backup/archive carveouts in DPA (retention/deletion)

Lena — adding a technical excerpt you can use directly as redline. Posting it here so legal can pick up verbatim if helpful.

Proposed excerpt (technical redline):
"Redwood will retain customer primary data in accordance with the retention schedule in Appendix A. Backups, snapshots, and archival copies are system artifacts kept for disaster recovery. Upon receipt of a verified erasure request, Redwood will: (i) mark affected snapshot identifiers for deletion; (ii) purge snapshot objects from active storage and mark tombstone entries in object metadata; (iii) schedule a background compaction job to remove residual blocks per storage provider semantics. Redwood will provide an Erasure Confirmation package within 30 business days for intra-region requests and within 60 business days for cross-region requests. Evidence package includes: list of snapshot ids removed, purge job ids, storage provider deletion receipts, and compacted object metadata showing tombstone entries. If data is encrypted with Customer-managed keys (BYOK), Redwood will include key rotation and access logs; however, Redwood cannot manipulate Customer-managed key material and will rely on customer cooperation to complete cryptographic erasure where applicable."

Notes: the "background compaction" wording helps set expectations for eventual physical block deletion; storage providers differ in how quickly blocks are reclaimed. Happy to walk through the evidence matrix on a quick screen share.

- Karthik
Principal Systems Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acmeinc.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Acme Legal <contracts@acmeinc.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Jul 2028 14:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on backup/archive carveouts in DPA (retention/deletion)

Ava / Karthik — thank you, this is helpful and more concrete than our original ask. A few follow-ups / counters from Acme: 

- Evidence SLA: Acme legal is comfortable with 30 business days for intra-region and 60 for cross-region, provided Redwoods Erasure Confirmation includes a hashed manifest we can validate and a short summary of storage provider receipts. Please include that manifest hash in the DPA appendix.

- Telemetry retention: We want the 30-day max for logs with PII to be a firm SLA (not an "exception list" backstop). Can Redwood commit to 30 days by default for any telemetry category flagged as potentially containing customer identifiers, with a process to request a longer retention (approval flows logged)?

- BYOK/onshore access: Acme will not provide private keys to Redwood. We need a clause that requires Redwood to notify and provide the audit trail within 72 hours of any lawful access attempt. The proposed wording about customer approval OR lawful access escalation is acceptable if the notice + audit trail commitment is explicit in the DPA.

If Redwood can add the manifest hash requirement and firm up the 30-day telemetry SLA, Acme legal is prepared to accept the rest of the backup redline and move to a DocuSign amendment. Ive attached our annotated comments again with inline acceptance marks (acme_dpa_comments.pdf).

We can sign off faster if you can provide the updated redline doc and a short confirmation email from Redwood legal on the manifest hash and telemetry SLA.

Best,

- Lena
Senior InfoSec Manager, Acme Inc.
attachments: acme_dpa_comments.pdf

Evidence package includes: list of snapshot ids removed, purge job ids, storage provider deletion receipts, and compacted object metadata showing tombstone entries. If data is encrypted with Customer-managed keys (BYOK), Redwood will include key rotation and access logs; however, Redwood cannot manipulate Customer-managed key material and will rely on customer cooperation to complete cryptographic erasure where applicable."

Notes: the "background compaction" wording helps set expectations for eventual physical block deletion; storage providers differ in how quickly blocks are reclaimed. Happy to walk through the evidence matrix on a quick screen share.

- Karthik
Principal Systems Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@acmeinc.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>

Date: Fri, 14 Jul 2028 14:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on backup/archive carveouts in DPA (retention/deletion)

Ava / Karthik — thank you, this is helpful and more concrete than our original ask. A few follow-ups / counters from Acme: 

- Evidence SLA: Acme legal is comfortable with 30 business days for intra-region and 60 for cross-region, provided Redwoods Erasure Confirmation includes a hashed manifest we can validate and a short summary of storage provider receipts. Please include that manifest hash in the DPA appendix.

- Telemetry retention: We want the 30-day max for logs with PII to be a firm SLA (not an "exception list" backstop). Can Redwood commit to 30 days by default for any telemetry category flagged as potentially containing customer identifiers, with a process to request a longer retention (approval flows logged)?

- BYOK/onshore access: Acme will not provide private keys to Redwood. We need a clause that requires Redwood to notify and provide the audit trail within 72 hours of any lawful access attempt. The proposed wording about customer approval OR lawful access escalation is acceptable if the notice + audit trail commitment is explicit in the DPA.

If Redwood can add the manifest hash requirement and firm up the 30-day telemetry SLA, Acme legal is prepared to accept the rest of the backup redline and move to a DocuSign amendment. Ive attached our annotated comments again with inline acceptance marks (acme_dpa_comments.pdf).

We can sign off faster if you can provide the updated redline doc and a short confirmation email from Redwood legal on the manifest hash and telemetry SLA.

Best,

- Lena
Senior InfoSec Manager, Acme Inc.

title: Hearthline hosting constraints & connectivity — post-call playback
preview:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ashley Grant <ashley.grant@hearthline.com>, Tom Morales <tom.morales@hearthline.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Jul 2028 16:12:00 +0000
Subject: Hearthline hosting constraints & connectivity — post-call playback

Thanks Ashley & Tom — great conversation this morning. Quick playback from my side and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth.

High-level goals we captured
- Move proof-of-concept to a private deployment (VPC) to keep PHI in-customer network and meet residency requirements.
- Maintain sub-100ms token latency for interactive clinician workflows where possible; batch async ingestion for analytics.
- Strong auditability (retention of request/response metadata) and key management integration.

Key constraints / blockers you emphasized
1) Network model: deployment must be air-gapped from the public internet by default. Outbound egress allowed only through a controlled NAT via customer-managed bastion or private peering to the vendor (no direct internet calls from inference hosts).
2) Data residency: All persistent logs and model artifacts must remain within Hearthline's AWS account in us-east-1. No backups to 3rd-party storage outside the account.
3) SOC2 evidence: you asked for a short extract of our SOC2 control mapping for data-at-rest, encryption-in-transit, and audit logging.
4) Procurement timeline: security review complete by end of month to meet Q3 pilot kickoff.

Immediate asks / actions we own
- I will share a network diagram draft that maps our recommended VPC architecture: private subnets for inference nodes, a small outbound egress path via customer NAT, and an optional S3 VPC endpoint for model syncs. (See attachment: hearthline-reqs-network-diagram-v1.png)
- Monica will pull the SOC2 control extract and a short readout mapping to the points you raised (encryption, logging, key management).
- Ben will prepare a slimmed-down procurement checklist (required firewall rules, service account roles, and expected lead times).

Proposed next steps and timeline
- Today (Jul 11): Deliver network diagram + SOC2 extract (Marissa / Monica).
- Jul 13: Short technical Q&A with your infra lead (30–45 min) to validate peering/NAT approach — suggested times attached in separate calendar invite.
- Jul 20: Draft SOW outline and pilot acceptance criteria (Ben).

Is there anything I missed or any detail you want spelled out differently? If the network model above is off, flag the exact constraint and we’ll update the diagram.

Thanks again — excited to get this moving.

—
Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachment: hearthline-reqs-network-diagram-v1.png (PNG, 1.2 MB)


From: Ashley Grant <ashley.grant@hearthline.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Morales <tom.morales@hearthline.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Jul 2028 09:08:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Hearthline hosting constraints & connectivity — post-call playback

Marissa — thank you, this is a helpful summary. A few clarifications: 

- Air-gap: we don't require full air-gap for the pilot; rather we need the ability to operate in an isolated mode with egress only to agreed endpoints. In practice that means: no outbound calls to unknown hosts, and all egress must be via our NAT with an allowlist.

- Model updates: we need to ensure model weights are stored in our S3 bucket and that Redwood's sync process operates from the bucket via an assumed role — we cannot allow model artifacts to be copied to vendor-managed buckets.

- Logging: fine to keep request metadata in-memory for real-time ops, but any long-term logs must be shipped to our Kinesis stream in-region under our account. Retention policy will be 90 days for anything containing PII.

I attached a short redlines doc to the calendar invite (not attached here) — will bring Tom in on the infra call. Confirming Jul 13 availability: 09:30–10:15 ET works for us.

Ashley
VP, Engineering
Hearthline Medical
ashley.grant@hearthline.com

On Thu, Jul 11, 2028 at 16:12 Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Ashley & Tom — great conversation this morning. Quick playback from my side and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth.
>
> [trimmed for inline reply]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ashley Grant <ashley.grant@hearthline.com>, Tom Morales <tom.morales@hearthline.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 13 Jul 2028 10:22:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Hearthline hosting constraints & connectivity — post-call playback

Ashley — perfect, thanks for the clarifications. Quick notes inline and what we'll bring to the infra call tomorrow: 

- Egress model: understood — we'll design the deployment with no internet-facing egress from inference hosts. All egress will be routable through your NAT and we'll provide a minimal allowlist of endpoints (model heartbeat, license check, metrics ingestion) that can be disabled for production if you prefer.

- Model syncs: we will implement an S3-based deployment flow where Redwood's deployment runner assumes an IAM role in Hearthline's account, pulls the model weights, and streams them directly to the inference nodes in the VPC. No copies to vendor-managed buckets. We'll capture the assumed role policy for your security review.

- Logs & retention: we can push structured operational logs to your Kinesis stream via an internal forwarder. We'll respect the 90-day retention and provide a separate write-up on PII scrubbing options before any persistent storage.

Attachments included: redwood-soc2-extract.pdf — control mapping (encryption in transit, KMS integration, audit logging).

Tomorrow's prep (Jul 13, 09:30–10:15 ET):
- Monica will run through the SOC2 mapping and KMS options (10–12 min)
- I'll demo the proposed VPC diagram and deployment flow (10–12 min)
- Ben will outline procurement/network rule asks and expected lead times (5–10 min)

If that timing still works, I'll send the calendar invite now.

—
Marissa

Attachment: redwood-soc2-extract.pdf (PDF, 240 KB)

Date: Sat, 13 Jul 2028 22:02:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Hearthline hosting constraints & connectivity — post-call playback

Ashley / Tom — thanks again for the call. A couple operational notes from procurement/op side so you have clarity before the infra sync: 

- Firewall rules: we'll provide a minimal ruleset. For the pilot you will need to allow outbound to the following host/IPs (these are our control plane egress endpoints) — we'll keep them static for the pilot: 52.23.114.7, 52.23.114.8 (US-East). If you will only allow DNS names, we can provide stable hostnames.

- Service accounts & roles: expected minimum is one IAM role for model-sync with S3 read access, and one role allowing write-only to your Kinesis stream for logs. We'll share a template CloudFormation/IaC snippet after the call.

- Timeline & gating: with security sign-off (SOC2 mapping + assumed-role review) we can stand up the pilot environment in ~2 weeks. If you need a full air-gapped, no-allowlist production variant, that adds custom engineering (estimate +4–6 weeks) — happy to scope that separately.

- Share IaC template + firewall rule list (within 24 hours).
- Draft pilot SOW & acceptance criteria (by Jul 20).

I'll drop the IaC snippet into the Drive folder and flag HS-345672 in your HubSpot record.

1) Network model: deployment must be air-gapped from the public internet by default. Outbound egress allowed only through a controlled NAT via customer-managed bastion or private peering to the vendor (no direct internet calls from inference hosts).
2) Data residency: All persistent logs and model artifacts must remain within Hearthline's AWS account in us-east-1. No backups to 3rd-party storage outside the account.
3) SOC2 evidence: you asked for a short extract of our SOC2 control mapping for data-at-rest, encryption-in-transit, and audit logging.
4) Procurement timeline: security review complete by end of month to meet Q3 pilot kickoff.

Immediate asks / actions we own
- I will share a network diagram draft that maps our recommended VPC architecture: private subnets for inference nodes, a small outbound egress path via customer NAT, and an optional S3 VPC endpoint for model syncs. (See attachment: hearthline-reqs-network-diagram-v1.png)
- Monica will pull the SOC2 control extract and a short readout mapping to the points you raised (encryption, logging, key management).
- Ben will prepare a slimmed-down procurement checklist (required firewall rules, service account roles, and expected lead times).

Proposed next steps and timeline
- Today (Jul 11): Deliver network diagram + SOC2 extract (Marissa / Monica).
- Jul 13: Short technical Q&A with your infra lead (30–45 min) to validate peering/NAT approach — suggested times attached in separate calendar invite.
- Jul 20: Draft SOW outline and pilot acceptance criteria (Ben).

Is there anything I missed or any detail you want spelled out differently? If the network model above is off, flag the exact constraint and we’ll update the diagram.

Thanks again — excited to get this moving.

—
Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0142

Marissa — thank you, this is a helpful summary. A few clarifications: 

- Air-gap: we don't require full air-gap for the pilot; rather we need the ability to operate in an isolated mode with egress only to agreed endpoints. In practice that means: no outbound calls to unknown hosts, and all egress must be via our NAT with an allowlist.

- Model updates: we need to ensure model weights are stored in our S3 bucket and that Redwood's sync process operates from the bucket via an assumed role — we cannot allow model artifacts to be copied to vendor-managed buckets.

- Logging: fine to keep request metadata in-memory for real-time ops, but any long-term logs must be shipped to our Kinesis stream in-region under our account. Retention policy will be 90 days for anything containing PII.

Hi Elena — appreciated, and thanks Ben for the intro. I reviewed the attached redlines and put together an internal-first pass. Summary below, then inline notes in the attached DPA_redline_lawyers.docx.

- Transfer-impact addendum: we can provide a one-page addendum summarising hosting regions, KMS options (customer-managed KMS supported in Dedicated/Private), and subprocessor snapshot. We'll include a narrow statement about retention and deletion windows.
- Subprocessors: we will supplement the DPA with a current subprocessor list and a commitment to 30 days' notice for new subprocessors. For Dedicated/VPC customers we can also include additional language limiting cross-region backups.
- Order form redlines: pricing redline comments look OK; however, the liability and warranty aggregation needs a short revision (see para 4 in our doc).

3) Ben / Karthik: please confirm the business dataset priority (Elena's earlier question) so we pick the right clause for telemetry vs PHI.
